description:
Infrastructure-only first slice of VR.3. Adds the typed-slot vocabulary to schemas without changing how codegen reads/writes them. Storage format stays tagged FzValue at every slot; GC tracer behavior is unchanged (typed-int/float slots still hold tagged ptrs, so tracing them is correct-but-redundant).

The win lands in .5.2/.5.3/.5.4/.5.5; this ticket is the substrate they share.

## Adds

- `build_frame_schema` takes a `Vec<FieldKind>` instead of `(name, num_entry_params)` — per-slot kind selection at construction time.
- Per-fn schema-build site (currently `schemas[f.id.0] = build_frame_schema(...)`) consults FnTypes to compute slot kinds for entry-frame params: `RawBytes(8)` for `int` / `float` Descrs, `FzValue` otherwise.
- Schema kind plumbed end-to-end so .5.2+ can read it.

## Kills

- The `(name: &str, num_entry_params: usize)` signature of `build_frame_schema`.
- The uniform-FzValue assumption baked into that function.

## Acceptance

- Existing tests + fixture matrix green (no behavior change yet).
- Schemas for fixtures with float/int entry params expose `RawBytes(8)` slots (verified via a unit test that builds a fn with annotated args).
- No CLIF deltas in `cargo run -- dump`.

## Blocks

- fz-ul4.27.5.2 (raw f64 storage), fz-ul4.27.5.3 (raw i64 storage)
title: VR.3.1: typed slot kinds (schemas-only infrastructure)
